```{r old-running-score}
            opp_goals = sum(match_goals$opp_goal[ match_goals$time_mins < time_mins],
                           na.rm = TRUE),
            goal_diff = lev_goals - opp_goals
          ) %>%
          ungroup()
      }
    }) %>%
    ungroup()
}

```

```{r press-height-for-old-teams}

# For each top-7 team, compute press height in the 4 DiD cells:
#   early (30–70') × losing / not-losing
#   late  (>70') × losing / not-losing
#
# We use the same restricted window (30–70') as our main analysis for consistency

LATE_THRESHOLD <- 70
EARLY_LOW_BOUND <- 30

compute_team_profile <- function(team_name, events_data, goals_data) {
  
  # Get press events for this team
  press <- events_data %>%
    filter(
      team.name == team_name,
      type.name == "Pressure",
      !is.na(location.x),
      !is.na(location.y)
    )
  
  if (nrow(press) < 50) {
    cat("  Too few press events for", team_name, "--> skipping\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Attach score state
  press_scored <- get_press_with_score(press, goals_data, team_name)
  
  # Build DiD variables and filter to restricted window
  press_did_team <- press_scored %>%
    mutate(
      goal_diff = as.integer(goal_diff),
      is_losing = goal_diff < 0,
      is_late = minute > LATE_THRESHOLD
    ) %>%
    filter(
      (minute >= EARLY_LOW_BOUND & !is_late) | is_late
    )
  
  # Compute cell means
  cell_means <- press_did_team %>%
    group_by(is_late, is_losing) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_x = mean(location.x, na.rm = TRUE),
      se_x = sd(location.x,   na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(team = team_name)
  
  cell_means
}

# Run for all top-7 teams
cat("Computing press profiles for 2015/16 top teams...\n")

profiles_1516 <- lapply(TOP_TEAMS_1516, function(team) {
  cat(" Processing:", team, "\n")
  compute_team_profile(team, events_1516, goals_1516)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

cat("\nProfile computation complete.\n")
cat("Teams successfully processed:",
    length(unique(profiles_1516$team)), "\n")

```

```{r metrics-calculation-for-old-teams}

# Compute the two key metrics per team:
#   1. losing_late_height: mean press height when losing after 70'
#   2. press_maintenance: losing-late height minus not-losing-late height
#   3. did_estimate: the full DiD β (arithmetic version)

team_metrics_1516 <- profiles_1516 %>%
  select(team, is_late, is_losing, mean_x, n) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = c(is_late, is_losing),
    values_from = c(mean_x, n),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    early_notlosing = mean_x_FALSE_FALSE,
    early_losing = mean_x_FALSE_TRUE,
    late_notlosing = mean_x_TRUE_FALSE,
    late_losing = mean_x_TRUE_TRUE,
    n_early_notlosing = n_FALSE_FALSE,
    n_early_losing = n_FALSE_TRUE,
    n_late_notlosing = n_TRUE_FALSE,
    n_late_losing = n_TRUE_TRUE
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Core metric: how high do they press when losing late?
    losing_late_height = round(late_losing, 2),
    
    # Maintenance: do they press higher when losing vs not losing, late game?
    press_maintenance = round(late_losing - late_notlosing, 2),
    
    # DiD: incremental shift above natural time trend
    did_estimate = round(
      (late_losing - early_losing) - (late_notlosing - early_notlosing), 2
    ),
    
    season = "2015/16"
  )

# Add Leverkusen 2023/24
lev_2324_metrics <- tibble(
  team = "Bayer Leverkusen\n2023/24 *",
  early_notlosing = early_notlosing,
  early_losing = early_losing,
  late_notlosing = late_notlosing,
  late_losing = late_losing,
  n_early_notlosing = as.integer(sum(!press_did_restricted$is_late &
                                      !press_did_restricted$is_losing)),
  n_early_losing = as.integer(sum(!press_did_restricted$is_late &
                                       press_did_restricted$is_losing)),
  n_late_notlosing = as.integer(sum( press_did_restricted$is_late &
                                      !press_did_restricted$is_losing)),
  n_late_losing = as.integer(sum( press_did_restricted$is_late &
                                       press_did_restricted$is_losing)),
  losing_late_height = round(late_losing, 2),
  press_maintenance = round(late_losing - late_notlosing, 2),
  did_estimate = round(did_estimate_r, 2),
  season = "2023/24"
)

# Combined table for all comparisons
all_metrics <- bind_rows(team_metrics_1516, lev_2324_metrics)

cat("--- Press Profile Metrics — Top Teams ---\n")
all_metrics %>%
  select(team, season, losing_late_height, press_maintenance,
         did_estimate, n_late_losing) %>%
  arrange(desc(losing_late_height)) %>%
  print()

```

```{r ranked-bar-chart, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}

# Figure 1: Ranked bar chart of losing-late press height
# Each bar = a team's mean press height when losing after 70'.

library(forcats)
library(stringr)

plot_bar <- all_metrics %>%
  filter(!is.na(losing_late_height)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_focal = team == "Bayer Leverkusen\n2023/24 *",
    team_label = str_wrap(team, width = 15),
    team_label = fct_reorder(team_label, losing_late_height)
  )

# Reference line: 2015/16 median
median_1516 <- median(
  all_metrics$losing_late_height[all_metrics$season == "2015/16"],
  na.rm = TRUE
)

fig_bar <- ggplot(plot_bar,
       aes(x = team_label, y = losing_late_height, fill = is_focal)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(losing_late_height, 1), "m")),
            hjust = -0.15,
            size = 3.8,
            colour = "grey20",
            fontface = "bold") +
  
  # 2015/16 median reference line
  geom_hline(yintercept = median_1516,
             linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey40",
             linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("text",
           x = 0.6,
           y = median_1516 + 0.8,
           label = paste0("2015/16 median\n(", round(median_1516, 1), "m)"),
           hjust = 0,
           size = 3.2,
           colour = "grey40",
           fontface = "italic") +
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "#7fbfcf", "TRUE" = "#d6604d"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(plot_bar$losing_late_height, na.rm = TRUE) + 8),
    breaks = seq(0, 120, 10)
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Counterpress Height When Losing After 70': Top Teams Compared",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Mean press location.x (m) in losing-late state | ",
      "Restricted window (30–70' pre-treatment)\n",
      "Red = Leverkusen 2023/24 | Blue = 2015/16 top-7 Bundesliga teams with enough data 
      available"
    ),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean Press Height (m from own goal line)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

print(fig_bar)
ggsave("fig_benchmark_losing_late_height.png", plot = fig_bar,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

Leverkusen 2023/24 (red) sits 5.5 m above the next-best team (Bayern 64.0 m) and 8.4 m above the 2015/16 median (61.1 m). This is a large gap on a pitch that has a total length of 120 m, and it is roughly equivalent to the width of the penalty area.

Notice how Leverkusen 2015/16 (53.0 m) falls below the 2015/16 median, confirming that the aggressive losing-late press is a 2023/24 phenomenon specific to Alonso's system, not a persistent club trait. Only 4 of the 7 top teams appear, provided that Schalke, Mainz, and Hertha had insufficient pressing event data in the losing-late cell to produce reliable estimates and were excluded from the figure.

```{r scatter-plot-for-teams, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}

# Figure 2: Scatter. Press maintenance vs losing-late height
# Two-dimensional view: x = how high they press when losing late,
# y = how much higher they press when losing vs not losing (late game).

plot_scatter <- all_metrics %>%
  filter(!is.na(losing_late_height), !is.na(press_maintenance)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_focal = team == "Bayer Leverkusen\n2023/24 *",
    team_short = case_when(
      grepl("2023/24", team)           ~ "LEV 23/24 *",
      grepl("Bayern", team)            ~ "BAY",
      grepl("Dortmund", team)          ~ "BVB",
      grepl("Leverkusen", team)        ~ "LEV 15/16",
      grepl("Gladbach|Mönchen", team)  ~ "BMG",
      grepl("Schalke", team)           ~ "S04",
      grepl("Mainz", team)             ~ "M05",
      grepl("Hertha", team)            ~ "BSC",
      TRUE ~ team
    )
  )

# Quadrant reference lines (medians of 2015/16 teams)
x_med <- median(plot_scatter$losing_late_height[plot_scatter$season == "2015/16"],
                na.rm = TRUE)
y_med <- median(plot_scatter$press_maintenance[ plot_scatter$season == "2015/16"],
                na.rm = TRUE)

fig_scatter <- ggplot(plot_scatter,
       aes(x = losing_late_height, y = press_maintenance)) +
  
  # Quadrant shading
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = x_med, xmax = Inf, ymin = y_med, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#d6604d", alpha = 0.06) +
  annotate("text",
           x = x_med + 0.5, y = max(plot_scatter$press_maintenance, 
                                    na.rm=TRUE) * 0.92,
           label = "High press +\nMaintains under pressure",
           size = 3, colour = "#d6604d", fontface = "italic", hjust = 0) +
  
  # Quadrant reference lines (2015/16 medians)
  geom_vline(xintercept = x_med, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = y_med, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid",
             colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # 2015/16 teams
  geom_point(data = filter(plot_scatter, !is_focal),
             colour = "#2166ac", size = 5, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(data = filter(plot_scatter, !is_focal),
            aes(label = team_short),
            vjust = -0.9, size = 3.3,
            colour = "#2166ac", fontface = "bold") +
  
  # Leverkusen 2023/24
  geom_point(data = filter(plot_scatter, is_focal),
             colour = "#d6604d", size = 7, shape = 18) +
  geom_text(data = filter(plot_scatter, is_focal),
            aes(label = team_short),
            vjust = -1.1, size = 3.8,
            colour = "#d6604d", fontface = "bold") +
  
  # n labels (sample size in treatment cell)
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("n=", n_late_losing)),
            vjust = 1.8, size = 2.8,
            colour = "grey50") +
  
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(50, 100, 5)) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Pressing Under Pressure: Two-Dimensional Team Profiles",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "x = mean press height when losing after 70' (higher = more aggressive)\n",
      "y = press height gap: losing-late minus not-losing-late (positive = maintains 
      press)\n",
      "Dashed lines = 2015/16 top-7 medians | n = treatment cell size"
    ),
    x = "Press Height When Losing Late (m)",
    y = "Press Maintenance Index\n(Losing Late − Not Losing Late, m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

print(fig_scatter)
ggsave("fig_benchmark_scatter.png", plot = fig_scatter,
       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)

```

The scatter places each team on two dimensions simultaneously: on the x-axis, raw press height when losing late (how aggressive overall) is denoted, whereas on the y-axis, press maintenance index (how much they elevate press when losing) is displayed.

Leverkusen 2023/24 (red diamond) sits in the extreme top-right quadrant, and it is the only team that combines both the highest absolute press height (69.5 m) and the strongest press maintenance (+8.80 m). No 2015/16 team occupies this quadrant with comparable values.

Dortmund (BVB) and Gladbach (BMG) show moderate maintenance but lower absolute height. Bayern Munich (BAY) presses high in absolute terms but shows relatively low maintenance (+2.52 m), suggesting their high press is a general style rather than a situational comeback response. Leverkusen 2015/16 (LEV 15/16) sits in the bottom-left, with low absolute height and negative maintenance. This is the sharpest possible contrast with their 2023/24 season.

```{r press-profile-did-plots, fig.width=12, fig.height=5, out.width="100%"}

# Figure 3: Press height profiles across all 4 DiD cells
# Shows each team's full pressing profile: early/late × losing/not-losing.

plot_profiles <- all_metrics %>%
  filter(!is.na(early_notlosing)) %>%
  select(team, season, is_focal = season,
         early_notlosing, early_losing,
         late_notlosing,  late_losing) %>%
  mutate(is_focal = team == "Bayer Leverkusen\n2023/24 *") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(early_notlosing, early_losing,
                  late_notlosing,  late_losing),
    names_to = "cell",
    values_to = "mean_x"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_late = grepl("late", cell),
    is_losing = grepl("losing", cell) & !grepl("not", cell),
    period = ifelse(is_late, "Late (>70')", "Early (30–70')"),
    score = ifelse(is_losing, "Losing", "Not Losing"),
    period_n = ifelse(is_late, 1, 0),
    team_short = case_when(
      grepl("2023/24", team)          ~ "LEV\n23/24*",
      grepl("Bayern", team)           ~ "Bayern",
      grepl("Dortmund", team)         ~ "Dortmund",
      grepl("Leverkusen",team)        ~ "LEV\n15/16",
      grepl("Gladbach|Mönchen", team) ~ "Gladbach",
      grepl("Schalke", team)          ~ "Schalke",
      grepl("Mainz", team)            ~ "Mainz",
      grepl("Hertha", team)           ~ "Hertha",
      TRUE ~ team
    ),
    team_short = fct_relevel(team_short, "LEV\n23/24*", after = Inf)
  )

fig_profiles <- ggplot(plot_profiles,
       aes(x = period_n,
           y = mean_x,
           colour = score,
           group = score)) +
  
  geom_line(aes(linewidth = is_focal, alpha = is_focal)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = is_focal), alpha = 0.9) +
  
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 0.8, "TRUE" = 1.8),
                         guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 0.5, "TRUE" = 1.0),
                         guide = "none") +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("FALSE" = 2, "TRUE" = 4),
                         guide = "none") +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Losing" = "#d6604d", "Not Losing" = "#2166ac"),
    name = "Score state"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("Early\n(30–70')", "Late\n(>70')"),
    expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 0.2))
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ team_short, ncol = 4) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Full DiD Cell Profiles: Leverkusen 2023/24 vs 2015/16 Top Teams",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Each panel = one team's mean press height across the 4 DiD cells\n",
      "Bold lines = Leverkusen 2023/24 | Look for steeper Losing slope in treatment 
      window"
    ),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean Press Height (m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

print(fig_profiles)
ggsave("fig_benchmark_profiles.png", plot = fig_profiles,
       width = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

In the figure above, each panel shows a team's mean press height across the 4 DiD cells. Leverkusen 2023/24 (bottom-left, bold lines) clearly displays an upward slope on the Losing line (+2.5 m early to late) while Not Losing drops sharply (-4 m). This creates the widening gap that defines comeback pressing. 

For the case of Bayern Munich, both lines are relatively flat and close together, which signals high general pressing intensity but little situational adjustment.

For the case of Dortmund, the losing line rises notably from ~60 m to ~64 m. This is the 2015/16 team with the most similar profile to Leverkusen 2023/24, though at a much lower absolute level.

Gladbach's lines converge late, as the gap between losing and not-losing narrows after 70', suggesting they do not differentiate by score state.

The Leverkusen 2015/16 is perhaps the most insightful panel, since the Losing line drops from early to late while Not Losing rises, producing a crossing pattern. Under Xabi Alonso's predecessor, losing late meant pressing lower, the complete tactical opposite of the 2023/24 system.

```{r extension-summary}

# Final summary table
cat("--- Benchmark Summary Table ---\n\n")
all_metrics %>%
  filter(!is.na(losing_late_height)) %>%
  arrange(desc(losing_late_height)) %>%
  mutate(
    percentile_rank = round(
      rank(losing_late_height) / n() * 100, 0
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    Team = team,
    Season = season,
    `Press ht (losing late)` = losing_late_height,
    `Press maintenance` = press_maintenance,
    `DiD estimate` = did_estimate,
    `N (losing late)` = n_late_losing,
    `Percentile rank` = percentile_rank
  ) %>%
  print()

```

---

# Conclusion

Our analysis investigated whether Bayer Leverkusen's 2023/24 unbeaten season was underpinned by an anomalous spatial pressing response under adverse conditions, or if their late-game aggression was a standard among elite Bundesliga teams. The spatial and econometric evidence hint at the former: under Xabi Alonso, Leverkusen engineered a distinctive, highly scalable tactical system defined by its extreme spatial escalation when chasing a deficit. By integrating spatial KDE heatmaps, zonal raster statistics, and a Difference-in-Differences (DiD) framework, several core tenets of their identity emerge:

- Precision Trap Geography: Leverkusen does not press blindly. Their defensive structure is designed to funnel opponents into the advanced flanks, where their pressing traps peak at a highly efficient 36% to 39% success rate.

- The Comeback Shift: When trailing after the 70th minute, Leverkusen breaks from natural game progression. Rather than dropping deeper as matches wear on, they enact a coordinated forward shift. While the aggregate DiD estimator (+1.36 m in the restricted 30–70 minute specification) is visually muted, conditioning the model on opponent quality reveals massive tactical escalation against mid-tier teams (+9.97 m). Against top-tier sides, they adopt a pragmatic stubbornness (+2.78 m), refusing to concede territory to dangerous counter-attacking opponents.

- A Historical Anomaly: Benchmarking this behavior against the top seven Bundesliga teams from the 2015/16 season with available data confirms that Alonso's system is an outlier, not an elite norm. Leverkusen 2023/24 posted the highest absolute press height when losing late (69.5 m) and the most aggressive press maintenance index (+8.80 m). Crucially, this is entirely a product of the current regime; the 2015/16 iteration of Leverkusen exhibited the exact opposite behavior, dropping their pressing lines significantly when behind.

We must transparently acknowledge the statistical boundaries of this study. The large p-values and wide confidence intervals in our DiD estimators fall short of traditional significance thresholds. However, in the context of sports analytics, this is a feature rather than a bug of an unbeaten season. Bayer Leverkusen simply spent so few late-game minutes in a losing state that achieving robust statistical power is mathematically impossible.

Ultimately, the spatial footprints left by this team tell a clear story. Bayer Leverkusen’s historic 2023/24 campaign was not just a product of technical brilliance or luck, but of a rigorously drilled, spatially anomalous tactical system that enclosed opponents exactly when the stakes were highest.

---
